# Build Plan Apply

Plan changes, apply generated work, compile platform artefacts, and verify generated output.

## kiri apply

Run ingestion → AI description → contracts...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri apply [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--project` | Project directory | no |  |  |
| `--target`, `-t` | Named profile to use (dev\|test\|prod). Falls back to KIRIMANA_TARGET or the project's default_target. | no |  |  |
| `--skip-dbt`, `--no-skip-dbt` | Skip the silver/gold dbt build step | no | False |  |
| `--skip-fetch`, `--no-skip-fetch` | Skip implicit REST/DATABASE fetch (use existing landing files) | no | False |  |
| `--contract` | Run only the named contract. Legacy sources-fallback is skipped; dbt build is skipped implicitly so the re-run stays local to the contract's own materialisation. Use the contract's `name` field, not the filename. | no |  |  |
| `--domain` | Run only contracts in the given domain. Matches the folder structure ``contracts/<domain>/...`` with medallion-layer subfolders skipped. dbt build + legacy-source fallback are skipped - one domain is a scoped re-run, same semantics as ``--contract``. | no |  |  |
| `--window` | Backfill a bounded watermark window '<START>..<END>' for the targeted flat watermark contract(s). OVERRIDES the store-derived window with an explicit upper of END. Without --reset it bounds this run's UPPER to END without rewinding (lower stays the stored cursor); with --reset it uses START as this run's lower bound so the range reprocesses (idempotent via the model's pre_hook window-delete). The cursor advances to END only on success. START/END are validated against the contract's watermark grain. A --window backfill MUST build dbt (it cannot be combined with --skip-dbt, --contract or --domain, which skip the dbt build): advancing the cursor without materializing the model would lose data, so the apply refuses rather than silently advance. | no |  |  |
| `--reset` | With --window: use the window START as this run's lower bound so the bounded range reprocesses. The stored cursor is NOT mutated up front - it advances to END only on a successful run, so a failed run leaves it unchanged. No effect without --window. | no | False |  |
| `--materialize` | Force the dbt build even on a scoped run (--contract / --domain), which otherwise skip it. This is what an orchestrator task (e.g. a scheduled Job running one contract/domain) uses so the run actually MATERIALISES its scope instead of succeeding as a silent no-op. No effect on a full (unscoped) apply, which always builds. | no | False |  |

## kiri compile

Compile contracts into runnable platform...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri compile [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

None.

### Subcommands

- [`kiri compile bronze`](#kiri-compile-bronze) - Compile every bronze contract under...
- [`kiri compile layer`](#kiri-compile-layer) - Compile per-(domain, layer) contracts to...
- [`kiri compile range`](#kiri-compile-range) - Compile a depth-range x breadth-selector...
- [`kiri compile silver`](#kiri-compile-silver) - Compile silver contracts to a DLT notebook...

### kiri compile bronze

Compile every bronze contract under...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri compile bronze [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--target` | Closed menu: dlt, copy-into, streaming-table, sql, dlt-with-apply-changes, dlt-with-append-flows. | yes |  |  |
| `--contracts` | Directory holding bronze ODCS YAML. | yes |  |  |
| `--output` | Output file. | yes |  |  |
| `--adapter` | Project target adapter (e.g. databricks, localduckdb). Required for --target dlt-with-apply-changes. | no |  |  |
| `--sql-dialect` | SQL dialect for --target sql row-hash expression. Closed menu: spark, duckdb. Default: spark. | no | spark |  |

### kiri compile layer

Compile per-(domain, layer) contracts to...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri compile layer [OPTIONS] LAYER
```

### Arguments

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `LAYER` | Closed menu: bronze \| silver \| gold \| all. 'gold' compiles to dbt-model SQL via the dbt-bridge gold generators (kiri.compile_target=sql); 'all' compiles bronze + silver + gold. | yes |  |  |

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--domain` | Domain name (directory under 01-config/). | yes |  |  |
| `--target` | Target binding declared in the layer contract (e.g. 'dev', 'prod'). | yes |  |  |
| `--out` | Output directory; artefacts land under <out>/<domain>/<layer>/. | no | 05-shadow-build |  |
| `--compile-target` | Override the layer contract's kiri.compile_target. Closed menu: sql, sql-full-snapshot, dlt-with-expectations, dlt-with-apply-changes, dlt-with-append-flows, view, materialized-view. | no |  |  |
| `--project-adapter` | Adapter name (e.g. 'databricks'). Required for compile_target=dlt-with-apply-changes. | no |  |  |
| `--sql-dialect` | SQL dialect for sql-full-snapshot row-hash (spark\|duckdb). | no | spark |  |
| `--project`, `-p` | Project directory. Defaults (in order) to KIRIMANA_PROJECT, the nearest kiri.yml above the cwd, then the `kiri use` active project. | no |  |  |

### kiri compile range

Compile a depth-range x breadth-selector...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri compile range [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--domain` | Domain name (directory under 01-config/). | yes |  |  |
| `--target` | Target binding declared in each layer contract (e.g. 'dev'). | yes |  |  |
| `--from` | Closed menu: bronze \| silver \| gold. Defaults to --upto when omitted. | no |  |  |
| `--upto` | Closed menu: bronze \| silver \| gold. Required if --from is given. | no |  |  |
| `--include` | Comma-separated fnmatch patterns of form <layer>.<table> (e.g. 'silver.SAT_*,bronze.raw_*'). Empty = include-all per layer in range. | no |  |  |
| `--exclude` | Comma-separated fnmatch patterns of form <layer>.<table>. | no |  |  |
| `--dv-kind` | Closed menu: hub \| link \| satellite \| reference. Filters silver DV tables. | no |  |  |
| `--dv-name` | fnmatch pattern on DV object name (e.g. 'customer_*'). | no |  |  |
| `--no-closure` | Refuse if the selected set is not closure-complete; report missing dependencies. Default = auto-expand the closure. | no | False |  |
| `--compile-target` | Override the layer contract's kiri.compile_target. Closed menu: sql, sql-full-snapshot, dlt-with-expectations, dlt-with-apply-changes, dlt-with-append-flows, view, materialized-view. | no |  |  |
| `--out` | Output directory; artefacts land under <out>/<domain>/<layer>/. | no | 05-shadow-build |  |
| `--project-adapter` | Adapter name (e.g. 'databricks'). | no |  |  |
| `--sql-dialect` | SQL dialect for sql-full-snapshot row-hash (spark\|duckdb). | no | spark |  |
| `--project`, `-p` | Project directory. Defaults (in order) to KIRIMANA_PROJECT, the nearest kiri.yml above the cwd, then the `kiri use` active project. | no |  |  |

### Examples

```bash
kiri compile range --domain <domain> --target dev --from silver --upto gold \
```

```bash
kiri compile range --domain <domain> --target dev --upto silver
```

```bash
kiri compile range --domain <domain> --target dev --upto silver \
```

### Usage

None.

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |

## kiri doctor

Run every project health check and print a...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri doctor [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--project`, `-p` | Project directory. Defaults to the discovered root. | no |  |  |
| `--target` | Named profile from kiri.yml. Enables live checks (adapter health). | no |  |  |
| `--only` | Comma-separated check ids to run exclusively. | no |  |  |
| `--skip` | Comma-separated check ids to skip. | no |  |  |
| `--format` | Output format: 'table' (default) or 'json'. | no | table |  |
| `--strict` | Treat warnings as failures (exit 2 on any warning). | no | False |  |

Show the task DAG + the lowered Databricks...

None.

| `--target` | Target env threaded into kiri apply. | no |  |  |

## kiri plan

Show what would happen on apply, and...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri plan [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--project` | Project directory | no |  |  |
| `--target`, `-t` | Named profile to use (dev\|test\|prod). Falls back to KIRIMANA_TARGET or the project's default_target. | no |  |  |
| `--strict`, `--no-strict` | Exit non-zero if any metadata conflict is detected. | no | False |  |
| `--format` | Output format: ``text`` (Rich console, default) or ``json`` (machine-readable summary; Sprint 1.2 G3). | no | text |  |
